steps.title: Use perpendicular slopes
steps.content:
A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. Since the tangent slope is 
$$
\frac{7}{4}
$$, the radius slope is
$$
-\frac{4}{7}
$$

steps.title: Write the radius slope
steps.content:
The radius goes from the center $(3,10)$ to the tangency point $(a,-5)$, so its slope is
$$
\frac{-5-10}{a-3}=\frac{-15}{a-3}
$$

steps.title: Solve for a
steps.content: Set the radius slope equal to $$ -\frac{4}{7} $$: $$ \frac{-15}{a-3}=-\frac{4}{7} $$ $$ -105=-4(a-3) $$ $$ a=\frac{117}{4} $$ The answer is D.
